author: @nppdcare
text:
The arrangement you have done seems like capacitor where you measure the voltage . 
But how can you prove that memristor stores previous resistance and value is stored . Please explain it .

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: refer to main youtube page and watch video 'on the point of the channel'

author: @mehtapozkan4395
text: The schematic shows the 2 Aluminium layers shorted. If this is true you are never measuring the resistance of the supposed memristor. You are measuring the internal resistance of the battery.

author: @GuitarCollector9
text: Thank you for sharing. :) I learned a lot.

author: @billunderwood3392
text:
Rob, did you see this? Creating cooling via two sheets of graphene laid 11.3 degrees misaligned, and hit with a particular (undisclosed nm) laser! Seems to me this would be a huge step forward, not just cooling computer chips but refrigeration in general.
http://www.manchester.ac.uk/discover/news/article/?id=12820

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: very cool - pun not intended lol

replies.author: @actudoran
replies.text: reminds me of the graphene molybdenum graphene sandwich ( with no contact ) apparently a very interesting combination for batteries.

replies.author: @actudoran
replies.text: judging by the valence number, wouldn't Molybdenum Chloride be a more potent electrolyte than AlCl3 ? ...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
actudoran
i guess - but it hydrolyzes readily to form HCl and is aggressively oxidative - not sure how you would handle it or even if it possible to form a stable system using it

replies.author: @actudoran
replies.text:
just a quick question ... I can get my mittens on iron chloride - apparently it is used for cleaning the cables / conductors ... 
Thing is that Fe is either 2 or 3 valences :) I don't know which one.
if I get it and it doubles my voltage ... 2 ... trebles it ... 3 then ... am I right ?

author: @keeganmaloney3440
text: great project! I love seeing you do projects most of us think that you would need a much higher grade lab to do. You are a great inspiration and encouragement to my own endeavors.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i firmly believe the only real thing you need is an enquiring mind mate

author: @arechikitsune
text: This Was awesome. I read in an article that graphene is hard to mass produce. Is that true?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it was but things have changed a lot in the last year or so

arechikitsune
